commits.message:
feat(site): real terminal via WebContainer

Replace in-browser Ink rendering with a real Node.js runtime via
WebContainer (StackBlitz). Users get an actual shell prompt where
they can run demos with `npx tsx demo.tsx`.

Architecture:
- coi-serviceworker enables SharedArrayBuffer on GitHub Pages
- WebContainer boots, mounts demo files, runs npm install
- jsh shell spawned and connected to xterm.js
- Selecting a demo writes demo.tsx and runs it
- Run button sends Ctrl+C + `npx tsx demo.tsx` to shell
- Editor changes auto-write demo.tsx (debounced 500ms)

Drops all Node polyfills (React/Ink/rich-js run inside WebContainer
now, not the browser) — bundle down from 3.1MB to 1.4MB.
